entries.title: Hospital Raid Starts Before Shigaraki Is Ready

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The heroes attack Jaku Hospital before Shigaraki’s enhancement process is complete, forcing Garaki and the Nomu infrastructure into crisis.
content.injection: At hospital-raid start, Shigaraki is not simply waiting as a finished boss; the heroes are trying to interrupt a process in progress.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

entries.title: Mirko Drives The Lab Assault Through Injury

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Mirko pushes through severe injury and isolation to reach the core lab, making her aggression and endurance central to the hospital front.
content.injection: Write Mirko’s raid scenes with ferocity and damage accumulation. She keeps moving through injury, not because she is unharmed.

entries.title: Garaki Is Dangerous Through Infrastructure Not Combat Skill

context.label: Doctor Garaki is exposed as PLF collaborator

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Garaki’s threat lies in hidden medical authority, Nomu production, body modification, and All For One infrastructure rather than frontline combat ability.
content.injection: Use Garaki as a systems villain. His danger comes from labs, secrets, and engineered monsters, not from hand-to-hand combat.

entries.title: High-End Nomu Are Independent Battlefield Threats

context.label: High-End Nomu awaken in the hospital lab

continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: High-End Nomu can think, regenerate, and fight at pro-hero levels, making them more dangerous than standard Nomu.
content.injection: When High-Ends awaken, treat them as coordinated lethal threats that can occupy top heroes and reshape the front.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

ability.name: High-End Nomu physiology
constraints: High-Ends can regenerate and reason but are still engineered beings.
antiLore: Do not write them as ordinary low-tier Nomu.

entries.title: Mirko Damaging The Tank Causes Imperfect Awakening

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Mirko damaging Shigaraki’s tank contributes to the incomplete and unstable timing of his awakening.
content.injection: Tie Shigaraki’s war state to an interrupted process. His power is catastrophic, but the enhancement was not completed cleanly.

entries.title: Present Mic Captures Garaki But Cannot Stop The Cascade Alone

context.label: Present Mic captures Garaki and disrupts the lab

content.fact: Present Mic captures Garaki and disrupts the lab, but the situation has already cascaded toward Shigaraki’s awakening.
content.injection: Present Mic’s capture of Garaki matters, but do not treat it as ending the threat once Shigaraki begins waking.

entries.title: Shigaraki Awakening Changes The War Scale

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Shigaraki’s awakening transforms the raid from a controlled operation into a catastrophic survival battle.
content.injection: After Shigaraki awakens, shift tone sharply: professional raid logic gives way to mass destruction, evacuation panic, and desperate containment.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

entries.title: Decay Wave Forces Immediate Evacuation And Flight

context.label: Shigaraki releases a massive Decay wave

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Shigaraki’s expanded Decay spreads destructively through connected surfaces, forcing heroes and civilians to escape upward or leave the ground.
content.injection: During the Decay wave, ground contact and debris chains are lethal. Survival should require flight, carrying others, or breaking contact with the spread.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

ability.name: Decay
constraints: Decay spreads through contact chains and terrain at this stage.
antiLore: Do not limit awakened Decay to one touched person during this Context.

entries.title: Crust Dies As A Professional Rescue Choice

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Crust dies saving Aizawa from the Decay wave, making his final act a rescue choice rather than just battlefield casualty.
content.injection: If Crust’s death is referenced, frame it around professional rescue and preserving Erasure for the battle, not a meaningless background death.

entries.title: Aizawa Becomes The Battle’s Critical Limiter

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Aizawa’s Erasure is the main reason Shigaraki cannot freely use all available Quirk power during the Jaku fight.
content.injection: When Aizawa has line of sight, Shigaraki is constrained. Protecting Aizawa should become a tactical priority for heroes.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

ability.name: Erasure
constraints: Erasure depends on Aizawa maintaining sight and eye function.
antiLore: Do not let Erasure work if Aizawa cannot see the target.

entries.title: Manual Supports Erasure By Keeping Aizawa’s Eyes Usable

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Manual’s support matters because keeping Aizawa’s eyes lubricated lets Erasure continue under extreme strain.
content.injection: Include Manual’s support when Erasure endurance matters. Aizawa is not maintaining the entire suppression alone.

entries.title: Quirk-Destroying Bullet Forces Aizawa’s Self-Sacrifice

context.label: Aizawa sacrifices his leg to stop a Quirk-destroying bullet

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: A Quirk-destroying bullet threatens Aizawa’s Erasure, and he sacrifices his leg to prevent losing his Quirk.
content.injection: In this moment, Aizawa’s choice should be brutal and immediate: preserving Erasure is worth bodily sacrifice because the battlefield depends on it.

entries.title: Shigaraki Body Is Enhanced But Not Stable

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Shigaraki’s body is massively enhanced, but the interrupted process and All For One influence make his condition unstable.
content.injection: Do not write awakened Shigaraki as simply perfected. He is catastrophic, but instability and internal conflict remain relevant.

entries.title: Endeavor Becomes Primary Firepower Against Shigaraki

content.fact: Endeavor’s Hellflame output becomes one of the heroes’ main ways to damage and pressure Shigaraki under Erasure.
content.injection: During Jaku, write Endeavor as heavy frontline firepower. He can injure Shigaraki but cannot solve the fight alone.

content.fact: Ryukyu’s dragon form gives the heroes aerial mobility, restraint, and protection during the Shigaraki fight.

content.fact: Shigaraki’s injury to Gran Torino connects the present battle to Nana Shimura’s family tragedy and mentor history.
content.injection: If Gran Torino is struck, frame it as more than damage: Shigaraki’s origin, Nana’s choices, and old hero failures are pressing into the fight.

content.fact: Shigaraki can identify One For All through Search, making Deku a direct target once Shigaraki awakens.
content.injection: When Shigaraki targets Deku here, tie it to One For All detection and All For One’s obsession, not random combat preference.

content.fact: Deku and Bakugo move toward Shigaraki partly to pull danger away from civilians and other evacuees once One For All is targeted.

content.fact: Destroying the lab damages villain infrastructure while also unleashing High-End threats before heroes can fully secure the site.

content.fact: Mirko’s wounds from the lab fight are severe and should persist through post-war continuity.
